ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acetin: noun. the triglyceride of acetic acid
ache: verb. To suffer pain; to be the source of, or be in, pain, especially continued dull pain; to be distressed.
aching: verb. present participle and gerund of ache
acing: verb. present participle and gerund of ace
acne: noun. A skin condition, usually of the face, that is common in adolescents.
act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
actin: noun. A globular structural protein that polymerizes in a helical fashion to form an actin filament (or microfilament).
acting: adjective. Temporarily assuming the duties or authority of another person when they are unable to do their job.

cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
cate: noun. A delicacy or item of food.

chia: noun. A Mexican sage grown for its edible seeds, Salvia hispanica.
chin: noun. The bottom of a face, the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
china: noun. Synonym of porcelain, a hard white translucent ceramic made from kaolin, now sometimes distinguished in reference to tableware as
chine: noun. The top of a ridge.
chit: noun. A child or babe; a young, small, or insignificant person or animal.
each: determiner. All; every; qualifying a singular noun, indicating all examples of the thing so named seen as individual or separate items
eight: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 8.
eng: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for English.

genic: adjective. of, relating to, produced by, or being a gene

ghat: noun. A descending path or stairway to a river; a ford or landing-place.
giant: noun. A mythical human or humanoid of very great size.
gie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guébie.
git: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gitxsan.

hang: verb. To be or remain suspended.
hant: noun. Alternative form of haunt, haint (“ghost”)
hat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Haitian Creole.

itch: noun. A sensation felt on an area of the skin that causes a person or animal to want to scratch said area.

theca: noun. Any of several external cases or sheaths.
thin: adjective. Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
